title: Accessibility in Design System
content: # Accessibility Systems Directories Components Articles Blueprints Guides Glossary Biweekly Digest Created by Color System Component lifecycle Component overview Component status Data visualization Elevation Figma Icons Illustrations International Design Layout Motion Patterns Repository Responsive Spacing States Storybook Styles Theming Tokens Typography Voice and Tone Keep up with the latest in Design Systems Subscribe Accessibility is the practice of creating inclusive experiences that can be accessed and used by people with diverse abilities. It involves considering the needs of individuals with disabilities, such as visual, auditory, motor, or cognitive impairments, and ensuring that digital products and interfaces are designed to accommodate these needs. Accessibility features may include alternative text for images, captions for videos, keyboard navigation, color contrast, and clear, concise content. By incorporating accessibility into design processes, products become more inclusive, allowing a wider range of users to engage with and benefit from digital experiences, ultimately fostering equality and equal access for all.
